title: baphia elegans var. vestita
intro: Baphia elegans var. vestita, also known as the Forest Pea, is a climbing shrub native to tropical Africa, belonging to the pea family Fabaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The Forest Pea is valued for its potential health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be taken internally or applied topically to harness its medicinal properties.
